title: Fundraising Ambassador
description: Help DAT grow its individual-donor base by sharing the story with your network. No cold calling — just warm connections and a genuine belief in what DAT does.
compensation: Volunteer
long_description: DAT's most effective fundraisers are the people who've been changed by the work — alumni, travelers, Drama Club families, friends. The Fundraising Ambassador formalizes what many of you already do: tell the story, make the introduction, invite someone into the community. We'll give you the tools, the talking points, and the support.
what_youll_do: Identify 5–10 people in your network who might connect with DAT
Share DAT's story through personal outreach or social media
Invite prospects to events, the alumni platform, or a direct conversation
Participate in a monthly Ambassadors call (optional but encouraged)
Report back on connections made
who_you_are: A genuine DAT believer — you've seen the work, been part of it, or been moved by it
Comfortable having personal conversations about causes you care about
Connected in arts, education, philanthropy, or community
requirements: DAT alumni or close community member preferred
perks: Direct briefings from Jesse on DAT's strategy and vision
Invitations to donor events and salons
Recognition in DAT's annual report
First look at new programs and projects

timeline.label: Monthly Ambassadors Call
timeline.detail: Optional; first Monday of the month

title: Alumni Outreach Captain
description: Help DAT reconnect with lost alumni, re-engage lapsed community members, and keep the network alive and growing. A role for someone with strong relationship instincts and a long memory.
compensation: Volunteer
long_description: DAT has thousands of alumni across every continent. Many haven't heard from us in years. The Alumni Outreach Captain helps us change that — making personal connections, sharing news, and inviting people back into the fold. This is a warm, relationship-first role for someone who loves people and understands what it means to belong to something.
what_youll_do: Identify alumni in your region or cohort who've gone quiet
Reach out personally — a message, an email, an introduction
Invite alumni to update their stories on the alumni platform
Flag high-engagement alumni who might be ready for a deeper role
Contribute to a quarterly Alumni Network report
who_you_are: A natural connector
A DAT alumnus or close community member with strong network instincts
Warm, persistent, and genuinely interested in people
requirements: Comfortable with email outreach and social media
perks: Invitations to alumni events, salons, and convenings
Quarterly briefings from the DAT team on programs, strategy, and upcoming projects
Recognition in DAT's network communications and annual report
Access to DAT's alumni and artistic network
Letter of reference available

title: Story Collector
description: Gather stories from DAT alumni, participants, and community members for the alumni storytelling platform and campaigns. A role for interviewers, writers, and people who love listening.
compensation: Volunteer
long_description: The alumni storytelling platform at stories.dramaticadventure.com is only as rich as the stories it holds. Story Collectors conduct short interviews with DAT alumni and participants, transcribe or summarize them, and hand them off to the Comms team for publishing. It's close listening work — and it matters.
what_youll_do: Conduct 1–2 story interviews per month (30–45 min, video or phone)
Transcribe or summarize interview content for the editorial team
Coordinate with the Comms team on story priorities and upcoming campaign themes
Keep a simple tracking log of interviews conducted and stories submitted
who_you_are: A good listener first
A writer or interviewer who knows how to draw people out
Patient and warm, genuinely curious about other people's experiences
requirements: Comfortable conducting video or phone interviews
perks: Published byline credit on DAT's storytelling platform (by arrangement)
Deep access to DAT's extraordinary global community
Writing and interview samples for your portfolio
Invitations to DAT events, salons, and convenings
Letter of reference available after consistent contribution

title: Event Volunteer
description: Support DAT events in New York — front of house, hospitality, setup, and audience experience. A great way to stay connected to the work and meet the community.
compensation: Complimentary event tickets
long_description:
DAT runs 20–30 events a year in NYC — performances, salons, fundraisers, community nights, donor gatherings. Behind every one is a team of volunteers who make the room feel alive. This is the entry point for many people into DAT's wider community.

Flexible commitment — come to one event or become a regular. Most volunteers do 2–4 events a season.
what_youll_do: Help with setup and breakdown
Greet audiences and check in ticket holders
Support hospitality — atmosphere, flow, guest experience
Help with merch and program distribution
Act as audience-experience host during shows
who_you_are: Friendly, reliable, and easy to be around
Comfortable on your feet for 3–4 hours
Love live performance and want to be near it
requirements: 18 years or older (16+ for non-alcohol events)
Available for at least one event per season
perks: Complimentary tickets to the event you work
Free tickets to two other DAT events per season
Dinner with the team at quarterly volunteer nights
First look at auditions and PLX opportunities

title: Grant Research Volunteer
description: Identify new grant and funding opportunities for DAT. Research foundations, government programs, and corporate sponsors that align with DAT's international, community-rooted mission.
compensation: Volunteer
long_description: DAT's Development team can't read every RFP. The Grant Research Volunteer helps fill the gap — scanning foundations, government programs, and arts funding databases for opportunities that match our work. You'll submit a monthly research brief that the Development team uses to prioritize its grant calendar.
what_youll_do: Research 3–5 new grant prospects per month using GrantStation, Foundation Directory, NEA, state arts councils, and other sources
Summarize each prospect in a short brief (eligibility, deadline, fit, notes)
Flag high-priority opportunities to the Development team
Maintain a running prospect tracker
who_you_are: Methodical and curious
Comfortable with database research
Interested in arts funding, nonprofit finance, or development as a field
perks: Real development experience — your research shapes DAT's grant calendar
Portfolio of development research work
Direct briefings from DAT's Development team on strategy and priorities
Access to DAT's alumni and artistic network
Invitations to DAT events and donor convenings
Letter of reference available after consistent contribution

title: Data / CRM Volunteer
description: Help DAT maintain clean, organized, and useful data across our donor, alumni, and participant databases. Ideal for someone with spreadsheet or CRM experience who wants to contribute with precision.
compensation: Volunteer
long_description: Clean data is the unglamorous foundation of everything DAT does — from donor stewardship to alumni engagement to grant reporting. The Data/CRM Volunteer helps us maintain it: deduplicating records, updating contact information, tagging and segmenting, and flagging inconsistencies.
what_youll_do: Audit and clean CRM records on a monthly basis
Update contact info, tags, and segmentation
Deduplicate alumni and donor records
Assist with data imports after events, campaigns, or program cycles
Flag systemic data issues to the Coordinator
who_you_are: Detail-oriented and careful
Comfortable with spreadsheets and CRMs
Not bothered by repetitive precision work
perks: Hands-on CRM and data management experience
Portfolio of data work for your professional profile
Access to DAT's team and network
Letter of reference available after consistent contribution

title: Program Recruitment Ambassador
description: Help DAT find the next wave of ACTion participants, Drama Club students, PLX applicants, and traveling artists. Share opportunities in your community, on your campus, or through your professional network.
compensation: Volunteer
long_description: DAT's programs fill from word of mouth. The Program Recruitment Ambassador formalizes that — sharing application deadlines, program descriptions, and personal recommendations with communities that should know about DAT's work. University contacts, community centers, arts organizations, theatre departments — your network is our pipeline.
what_youll_do: Share DAT opportunity announcements with your network, on campus, or in your community
Identify organizations and institutions to add to DAT's outreach list
Post or share program deadlines on social media
Participate in a quarterly recruitment check-in call
who_you_are: Plugged into a community where DAT's programs would resonate
Enthusiastic about DAT's mission
Natural sharer
requirements: Active in a relevant community or institution
perks: First look at new program announcements and season planning
Invitations to DAT events, salons, and convenings
Access to DAT's global alumni and artistic network
Recognition in program materials and DAT communications
Letter of reference available after consistent contribution

title: Travel & Logistics Research Volunteer
description: Support DAT's producing team by researching housing, transport, visa requirements, venues, and logistics for upcoming expeditions and residencies.
compensation: Volunteer
long_description: Planning an international theatre expedition requires a lot of ground-level research — housing options, local transport, visa requirements, cultural considerations, local contacts. The Travel & Logistics Research Volunteer helps our producing team build the picture before the decisions get made. Project-based, you'll be assigned specific research tasks with clear deliverables and deadlines.
what_youll_do: Research housing options for a specific expedition (DAT typically houses 6–15 artists)
Map transport logistics — airport transfers, local travel, vehicle hire
Summarize visa requirements for a mixed-nationality ensemble
Identify potential local partners, venues, or service providers
Submit a structured research brief to the Managing Producer
who_you_are: Methodical, resourceful, comfortable with international research
May have experience with travel logistics, event production, or study abroad
requirements: Comfortable with online research in English and other languages (with tools)
Available for a 2–3 week project window
perks: Real producing and logistics experience — your research directly informs DAT's field decisions
Portfolio of international logistics research
Insight into the actual infrastructure of international theatre-making
Access to DAT's alumni and artistic network
Invitations to field briefings and producing convenings
Letter of reference available after consistent contribution

timeline.label: Project-based
timeline.detail: Assigned as expedition planning windows open

title: Community Partner Liaison
description: Help DAT understand the community landscape in cities where we work. A connector role for people with strong local ties who want to support DAT’s relationship-building — without owning decisions.
compensation: Volunteer
long_description: DAT’s partnerships are the arteries of the organization — local drama clubs, community centers, universities, cultural organizations, arts agencies. The Community Partner Liaison helps us understand and navigate those landscapes in cities and regions where we work or plan to work. DAT staff own the formal relationship-building and any institutional commitments; the Liaison helps us see clearly. You know the landscape; we’ll give you the context.
what_youll_do: Research and surface relevant organizations, venues, and community contacts in your hub
Attend local arts or community events and share what you learn with the DAT team
Keep a simple running log of promising relationships and local context
Pass along warm introductions between people in your network and DAT staff
Share occasional updates on the arts and community landscape in your region
who_you_are: Deeply embedded in your local arts, education, or community sector
A natural bridge-builder with a warm reputation
Patient with relationship timelines
requirements: Strong local network in a city or region relevant to DAT
Comfortable representing an organization in community settings
perks: DAT community access and deep insight into our regional partnerships
Invitations to relevant DAT programs, events, and salons in your region
Access to DAT's global alumni and artistic network
Recognition in regional partnership materials
Letter of reference available after consistent contribution

title: Traveling Ensemble Artist Roster
description: Join DAT's artist bench for future international ensemble projects. Paid when placed on a project; rate varies by project scope and funding. This is an ongoing roster — not a call for a specific project.
compensation: Paid when placed; rate varies by project and funding
long_description:
DAT builds ensembles of traveling artists for each major international project — PASSAGE, ACTion, and CASTAWAY productions. When a new project opens, we draw from this roster to invite artists into the ensemble. Placement is project-specific and based on artistic fit, availability, and the needs of each community we’re working with.

This listing is for the ongoing evergreen roster. If you’re looking for the current open call — PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 — see that listing directly.
what_youll_do: Devise and perform original work with an international ensemble
Co-lead community workshops with local youth and community members
Adapt your practice to non-traditional performance spaces and cultural contexts
Contribute to documentation and storytelling for the project
Participate in post-residency sharings and presentations
who_you_are: An experienced ensemble performer or devisor
Comfortable working across language and culture
A listener first — genuinely interested in other people’s stories
Ready to be changed by the work
requirements: Experience in devised, physical, or community-engaged performance
Comfortable living and working in ensemble housing
Available to commit to the full in-field window of any project you’re placed on
perks: Paid when placed; artist fee rate confirmed upon engagement
Housing and logistics supported for the in-field period
Portfolio documentation
DAT alumni artist network

timeline.label: Applications Reviewed
timeline.detail: Rolling — we review when new projects open

timeline.label: Project-Specific Timelines
timeline.detail: Shared directly when a match is made

faq.q: What’s the difference between this roster and PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026?
faq.a: PASSAGE: Slovakia 2026 is a specific upcoming project with an artist participation fee model — you pay to participate. The Traveling Ensemble Artist Roster is for future paid ensemble projects where DAT places artists on contract. Apply to both if you’re interested in either.
We build the piece together from scratch — no pre-existing script. Come with stories, movement, and questions.

faq.q: How often do ensemble projects happen?
faq.a: Roughly one to two international ensemble projects per season. DAT is expanding its project calendar as it builds toward Season 21.

title: Actor / Devising Performer
description: Actors and devising performers for DAT's international productions, ACTion expeditions, and CASTAWAY co-productions. Open call for the general roster — applied to productions as they open.
compensation: Paid when placed; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT casts from a standing roster of actors and devising performers — pulled when the right project opens. Join the roster with a strong bio, work samples, and a note about your practice. We cast across genres, training backgrounds, and career stages. What we care about: ensemble instinct, story generosity, and the ability to work in the unknown.
what_youll_do: Submit bio, headshot/reel, and a short note on your practice
Get matched to relevant casting calls
Audition or interview when a project fits
Join an ensemble, make something together
who_you_are: An actor or devising performer with ensemble instincts
Comfortable with physical, text-based, or hybrid devising approaches
Genuinely interested in community-engaged and international contexts
requirements: Passport and ability to travel internationally preferred
perks: Artist fee when placed
First look at casting calls
DAT alumni artist network
Invitation to DAT artist events and gatherings

title: Director / Facilitator
description: Directors and facilitators for DAT's devised productions, ACTion expeditions, and community-engaged residencies. General roster — matched to projects as they open.
compensation: Paid when placed; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT works with directors and facilitators who can hold a devising room, build community trust, and navigate the beautiful complexity of making original work across cultures. If you have a practice at the intersection of directing and facilitation — and if you're drawn to international, community-rooted work — we want to know about you.
what_youll_do: Submit portfolio, bio, and a note on your practice
Get matched to directing and facilitation opportunities as they open
Work with DAT's artistic team to shape and direct an original piece
Lead ensemble and community facilitation sessions
who_you_are: An experienced director or facilitator with devising and/or community-engaged experience
Culturally humble, flexible, and at ease in the unknown
Strong listener and room-holder
requirements: Portfolio of directing or facilitation work
Ability to travel internationally for project periods
perks: Director fee when placed
DAT alumni artist network
Invitation to DAT artistic convenings

title: Playwright / Adaptor
description: Playwrights and adaptors for DAT's original productions and CASTAWAY co-productions. General roster — matched to commissions and adaptation projects as they open.
compensation: Commission fee; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT commissions original scripts and adaptations for its productions — often derived from community stories, historical research, and in-the-field devising. We're building a roster of playwrights and adaptors who understand community-engaged dramaturgical practice and who can work inside a collaborative creative process. If you write from listening, we want to know about you.
what_youll_do: Submit portfolio and a note on your practice
Get matched to commission and adaptation opportunities
Collaborate with the artistic team on source material and development
Attend in-field research or residency when relevant
Deliver draft texts on production timelines
who_you_are: A playwright or adaptor with a community-rooted or world-theatre lens
Comfortable with collaborative development and dramaturgical revision
Strong voice, strong ear, strong process
requirements: Ability to travel for research or residency when relevant
perks: Commission fee when placed
Publication/documentation of your work
DAT alumni artist network

title: Dramaturg / Researcher
description: Dramaturgs and researchers for DAT's active productions and residencies. Deep knowledge of cultural context, world theatre, or community-based practice especially valued.
compensation: Paid when placed; rate varies by project
long_description:
DAT's productions are research-rich — built from history, community stories, cultural context, and textual adaptation. We work with dramaturgs who can hold the intellectual and ethical weight of that research, translate it for an ensemble, and stay inside the creative process as the work evolves.

If you have deep knowledge of Central Europe, Latin America, East Africa, or another DAT hub region, we especially want to hear from you.
what_youll_do: Conduct contextual research for a specific production or residency
Build and maintain a dramaturgical archive
Present research to the ensemble in accessible, actionable form
Advise on cultural accuracy, representation, and ethical storytelling
Attend rehearsals or workshops when relevant
who_you_are: A dramaturg or researcher with experience in community-engaged or world-theatre contexts
Intellectually rigorous and creatively flexible
Committed to ethical representation of community stories
requirements: Language skills or deep cultural knowledge of DAT's hub regions is a significant plus
perks: Dramaturgical fee when placed
Credits on production materials
DAT alumni artist network

title: Teaching Artist
description: Teaching artists for DAT's Drama Clubs, school residencies, and community storytelling programs. We place teaching artists locally and internationally — across NYC, Slovakia, Ecuador, and beyond.
commitment: Varies by project (semester, residency, or workshop-based)
compensation: Teaching artist fee; rate varies by program
long_description:
DAT's teaching artist practice is the heartbeat of the organization — rooted in Drama Club seasons, school residencies, community storytelling workshops, and the training of local teaching artists in each hub we work in.

We place teaching artists who are genuine artists first, who bring their practice into rooms with young people and community members, and who understand the difference between teaching skills and opening possibility. If you are a teaching artist — or an artist who wants to develop a teaching practice — we want to know about you.
what_youll_do: Lead Drama Club sessions, school residencies, or community workshops
Design curriculum in collaboration with DAT's education team
Develop youth and community voices through storytelling, devising, and performance
Document your teaching practice for DAT's educational archive
Participate in DAT's teaching artist training and professional development
Travel to international hubs if relevant and available
who_you_are: An artist first — a practitioner with a real creative practice
Warm, patient, and energized by working with young people and communities
Comfortable in both structured and open-ended educational settings
Interested in developing and refining your teaching artist pedagogy
requirements: Experience as a teaching artist or artist-educator
Background check required for youth-serving programs
Passport and willingness to travel for international placements
perks: Teaching artist fee (rate by program)
International placement opportunities
Formal pedagogy training through DAT
DAT alumni teaching artist network
Letter of recommendation available

timeline.label: Placement
timeline.detail: When the right program opening matches your profile

faq.a: Helpful but not required. We train you on DAT's curriculum frameworks.
Yes. Travel and housing are included for international residency placements.

title: Local Artist / Cultural Collaborator
description: Local artists, cultural practitioners, and community members who partner with DAT's visiting ensembles. Based in one of DAT's hub cities — Slovakia, Ecuador, Tanzania, or beyond — and open to making work across cultures.
compensation: Collaboration fee; rate varies by project
long_description:
DAT's work only works because of local artists and cultural collaborators. When we arrive in Bratislava, Quito, or Bagamoyo, we come to listen — and local artists are the people we listen to first and most.

This call is for artists, cultural practitioners, community leaders, and creative people who are based in a DAT hub region and open to collaborating with an international ensemble. The relationship is reciprocal and honest.
what_youll_do: Introduce DAT's visiting ensemble to your community, practice, and place
Co-create material for an original devised piece
Lead or co-lead a community workshop from your own practice
Serve as a cultural guide and creative collaborator throughout the residency
Perform in or co-present the final work if relevant and desired
who_you_are: A local artist, cultural practitioner, community leader, or creative person based in a DAT hub region
Genuinely interested in cross-cultural creative exchange
Comfortable in collaborative and ensemble settings
requirements: Based in or deeply connected to a DAT hub region
Ability to communicate in English (with support) or in the local language with a translator
perks: Collaboration fee when engaged
Documentation and portfolio materials
Cross-cultural artistic exchange
DAT alumni international network

timeline.label: Matched
timeline.detail: When the right project in your region opens

faq.q: Which regions does DAT currently work in?
faq.a: Slovakia/Czechia, Ecuador, Tanzania, and NYC are our primary active hubs. We're scouting Australia and other regions.

title: Designer
description: Designers for DAT's productions — set, costume, lighting, sound, or projection. We work in non-traditional spaces and value designers who can make meaningful work with limited resources.
compensation: Design fee; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT makes theatre in fields, courtyards, community centers, and found spaces around the world. Our designers are creative problem-solvers who work with the space, not against it — and who find beauty in constraints. We're building a roster of designers across disciplines for our upcoming productions and residencies.
what_youll_do: Submit portfolio and design statement
Get matched to design opportunities on upcoming productions
Collaborate with the director and ensemble on design concept
Execute design within production constraints (budget, space, travel)
Travel in-field for applicable productions
who_you_are: A designer with experience in non-traditional, site-specific, or touring contexts
Resourceful and collaborative
Comfortable with ambiguity and design constraints
requirements: Ability to travel for applicable productions
Experience designing for non-traditional spaces preferred
perks: Design fee when placed
Portfolio documentation
DAT alumni artist network

title: Stage Manager
description: Stage managers for DAT's productions — NYC performances, ACTion expeditions, and international residencies. Comfort with non-traditional spaces, devised process, and international work is essential.
compensation: SM fee; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT's stage managers are the production's center of gravity — holding the process together across devising, community workshops, technical rehearsals, and performance. We work in unusual spaces, across cultures, and often without a full technical infrastructure. If you bring rigor to the unexpected, we want to know about you.
what_youll_do: Stage manage rehearsals, community workshops, and performances
Build and maintain schedules, contact sheets, and run-of-show documents
Coordinate with designers, technical staff, and artists
Call the show, manage the deck, manage the process
Travel in-field when applicable
who_you_are: An experienced stage manager — preferably with devised or non-traditional experience
Precise, calm, and relationship-forward
Comfortable managing process, not just performance
requirements: Ability to travel internationally for applicable productions
perks: SM fee when placed
DAT alumni artist network
Production credits

title: Musician / Composer / Sound Artist
description: Musicians, composers, and sound artists for DAT's productions and residencies. We work with live music, original composition, and sound design — often in community and site-specific contexts.
compensation: Artist fee; rate varies by project
long_description: Music and sound are central to DAT's work — not as decoration, but as language. We work with musicians who can compose, improvise, teach, and perform in the same breath, and with sound artists who understand the texture of a room, a community, a culture. We're building a roster of musicians, composers, and sound artists for our upcoming productions and residencies.
what_youll_do: Submit bio, work samples, and a note on your practice
Get matched to musical or sound design opportunities
Compose or arrange original music for a production
Lead a music-based community workshop
Travel in-field for applicable productions
who_you_are: A musician, composer, or sound artist with experience in theatre, community, or site-specific contexts
Comfortable improvising and collaborating
Interested in cross-cultural musical exchange
requirements: Ability to travel internationally for applicable productions
perks: Artist fee when placed
Documentation and portfolio materials
DAT alumni artist network
Cross-cultural musical exchange

title: Movement Director / Choreographer
description: Movement directors and choreographers for DAT's devised productions and physical theatre work. We value movement practitioners who work from the body and the community — not just the stage picture.
compensation: Artist fee; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT's productions are physical — they live in the body before they live on the page. We work with movement directors and choreographers who can work in ensemble, across cultures, and with non-professional community members as well as trained performers. If your practice is rooted in the body as a storytelling instrument, we want to hear from you.
what_youll_do: Lead movement work with ensemble and community participants
Develop physical vocabulary for an original production
Teach and facilitate movement workshops
Collaborate with the director on staging and physical language
Travel in-field for applicable productions
who_you_are: A movement director or choreographer with ensemble and/or community-engaged experience
Committed to accessibility and invitation in physical work
Comfortable working cross-culturally
requirements: Portfolio of movement direction or choreography
Ability to travel internationally for applicable productions
perks: Artist fee when placed
Documentation and portfolio materials
DAT alumni artist network

title: Visual Artist / Maker / Artisan
description: Visual artists, makers, and artisans who want to collaborate with DAT on set, props, installations, or community-art projects. We especially value handcraft, site-specificity, and work made from local materials.
compensation: Artist fee; rate varies by project
long_description: DAT's productions live in real places — and we want the physical world of the show to live there too. We work with visual artists, makers, and artisans who can build set elements, props, installations, and community-art pieces from local materials, in local traditions, in collaboration with the people who live there. If you make things with your hands and you're curious about what art can do in a community, we want to hear from you.
what_youll_do: Collaborate with DAT's design team on visual and physical elements of a production
Source and work with local materials and artisans
Lead a making workshop with community members
Build installation, set, or prop elements in the field
Contribute to documentation of the making process
who_you_are: A visual artist, sculptor, maker, or artisan with community or site-specific experience
Comfortable working with limited resources in unusual spaces
Generous collaborator
requirements: Portfolio of visual or making work
Ability to travel for applicable productions
Comfort with physical labor and hands-on making
perks: Artist fee when placed
Documentation and portfolio materials
DAT alumni artist network

title: Documentation Artist
description: Photographers, videographers, and documentary storytellers for DAT's productions and residencies. We want documentation that captures the truth of the work — not just the performance.
compensation: Artist fee; rate varies by project
long_description:
DAT's work happens in fields, studios, community centers, and living rooms around the world — and most of it is never seen by anyone outside the room. The Documentation Artist changes that.

We're building a roster of photographers, videographers, and documentary storytellers who want to capture the real texture of the work — the listening, the rehearsal, the moment before the show, the meal afterward. If you can make a frame that tells the truth, we want to hear from you.
what_youll_do: Document productions, workshops, and community sessions in the field
Build a project archive — photo, video, audio
Collaborate with the Comms team on content production
Develop your own documentary project around the work
Contribute to DAT's permanent institutional archive
who_you_are: A photographer, videographer, or documentary storyteller
Comfortable working in community and performance settings
Thoughtful about the ethics of documentation
requirements: Portfolio of documentary photography or video
Ability to travel in-field for applicable projects
perks: Artist fee when placed; rate confirmed per project
Published portfolio work — photos, video reels, written documentation — with DAT attribution
In-field housing and logistics support may be included depending on the project
Field travel tied to confirmed project placements; logistics discussed before engagement
DAT alumni and artist network access

title: Board Committee Member
description: Serve on a DAT Board committee — Finance, Governance, Development, or Artistic Advisory. Governance-level engagement without full Board commitment. For people who believe in DAT’s mission and bring relevant expertise.
compensation: Volunteer
long_description:
DAT’s Board committees are where mission meets expertise. We’re building committees in Finance, Governance, Development, and Artistic Advisory — each a focused group of people who bring real knowledge and care to DAT’s institutional work.

Committee members are not full Board members. They advise, review, and contribute on specific questions — without the full fiduciary commitment of Board service. This is a meaningful, low-overhead way to support an organization doing unusual work.
what_youll_do: Attend committee meetings (typically 4–6 per year, virtual)
Review materials relevant to your committee’s focus (finance reports, governance docs, grant proposals, etc.)
Provide expert input, ask hard questions, and help DAT think clearly
Connect DAT staff to relevant contacts in your network where appropriate
Support the full Board’s decision-making with committee recommendations
who_you_are: A professional with expertise in finance, law, governance, development, or the arts
Genuinely interested in DAT’s mission and the sector it works in
Willing to engage seriously with the organization’s real challenges
No formal governance or Board experience required — orientation provided
requirements: Ability to attend virtual committee meetings (approximately monthly)
perks: Meaningful governance engagement without full Board commitment
DAT community access and invitations to programs, salons, and artistic convenings
Direct engagement with DAT's Artistic Director and Board leadership
Access to DAT's global alumni and artistic network
Letter of reference available

timeline.label: Rolling
timeline.detail: Join a committee anytime — next meeting scheduled upon onboarding

faq.a: Finance, Governance, Development, and Artistic Advisory. Tell us your background and we’ll suggest the right fit.

faq.q: Does this lead to a full Board seat?
faq.a: It can, but it doesn’t have to. Many committee members stay as committee members indefinitely.

title: Host Committee Member
description: Lend your name, network, and enthusiasm to a DAT event, campaign, or fundraiser. Help us reach people we wouldn’t otherwise reach — by making a personal ask, sharing the event, or simply showing up.
compensation: Volunteer
long_description:
DAT’s host committees are loose, event-specific networks of people who believe in the work and are willing to say so publicly. For each major event or campaign, we invite a small group to lend their names, share the event with their networks, and make a personal ask to a few people they think would care.

This is not a standing commitment. Each host committee is formed for a specific purpose and dissolves when the event or campaign ends. You opt in each time.
what_youll_do: Lend your name to a specific DAT event, fundraiser, or campaign
Share the event with people in your network via email or personal message
Make a personal ask to two or three people you think would genuinely respond
Attend the event or campaign launch where possible
who_you_are: Someone who believes in DAT’s work and is willing to say so
Connected to people who might attend, donate, or get involved
Comfortable making a personal (not mass) ask on behalf of something you believe in
requirements: No formal requirements — this is about your enthusiasm and your network
Willingness to make a personal ask to at least two or three people
perks: Invitations to all DAT events you help host
DAT community access and social inclusion at events
Recognition in event materials (optional)
Access to DAT's alumni and artistic network

timeline.label: Rolling
timeline.detail: We invite host committee members on a per-event basis — let us know you’re interested and we’ll reach out when the next one comes together

faq.a: No. Each host committee is event-specific. You opt in each time — no ongoing obligation.

faq.q: Do I need to make a donation?
faq.a: Not required, but appreciated. The most valuable thing is your personal outreach to people who wouldn’t otherwise hear about DAT.

title: Ecuador Local Producer / Ground Coordinator
description: Project-based producing and logistics coordination for DAT programs in Ecuador — ACTion expeditions, Drama Club seasons, Teaching Artist Residencies, and field work across the country. Paid when active. Bilingual Spanish/English required. Part of DAT's Ecuador Founding Hub Team.
commitment: Project-based (varies by program; typically 1–3 months per engagement)
compensation: Paid when placed; project contract fee varies by program and funding. In-field housing, meals, or in-kind support may be included depending on the project; all logistics expectations are discussed before engagement.
long_description:
When DAT is active in Ecuador — an ACTion expedition, a Drama Club season, a Teaching Artist Residency, a community performance — we need a producer on the ground who knows the territory.

The Ecuador Local Producer / Ground Coordinator is a project-based role: you come in for a specific engagement, own the local logistics, and leave with a strong credit and a standing invitation to come back. We're building a reliable bench of Ecuador-based producers as DAT grows its year-round Ecuador footprint.

Paid when active; contract fee varies by project and funding. In-field housing, meals, or other in-kind support may be included depending on the program. Travel, out-of-pocket expectations, and logistics support are discussed clearly before engagement begins.

This role is part of DAT's Ecuador Founding Hub Team.
what_youll_do: Own local logistics for a specific DAT program in Ecuador — housing, venues, transport, hospitality, permits
Coordinate with local community partners, schools, artists, and venues
Manage the local project budget and logistics timeline in partnership with DAT's Managing Producer
Serve as primary liaison between DAT HQ and on-the-ground reality during the program
Support artist arrivals, orientations, and day-to-day production needs
Document the program for DAT's records and storytelling channels
Build and maintain local vendor and partner relationships between engagements
who_you_are: Based in Ecuador — you know Quito well and have working knowledge of DAT's program regions
Bilingual Spanish/English — essential for this role
A practical, resourceful producer — you've managed logistics under pressure and solve problems before they become crises
Warm and bridge-building — you connect DAT artists with communities with care and cultural intelligence
Familiar with Ecuador's arts, education, or community organization landscape
requirements: Based in Ecuador; Quito-based strongly preferred
2+ years of producing, event management, project coordination, or community organizing experience
Ability to commit fully to a project during the active program period
References from prior production or community work
perks: Paid when placed; project contract fee confirmed per engagement
In-field housing, meals, or in-kind support may be included depending on the program
Professional title and production credits on DAT programs
DAT alumni and artist network access
First right of consideration for future DAT Ecuador engagements
Portfolio documentation from each program
Invitations to DAT field briefings, artistic convenings, and community events

timeline.label: Evergreen Roster
timeline.detail: Express interest now; we'll match you when the right project opens

timeline.label: Next Ecuador Programs
timeline.detail: 2026–2027 season; specific dates to be confirmed

faq.a: No — it's project-based. You're engaged for a specific program, then we stay in close touch for the next one.

faq.q: Do I need to speak both Spanish and English?
faq.a: Yes — bilingual Spanish/English is required. You'll be communicating with DAT HQ (US-based) and with local Ecuadorian partners and communities.
